cv.title: How to Write a CV
cv.subtitle: Master the art of CV writing with our comprehensive guide.
cv.intro: A well-crafted CV is your ticket to landing interviews. Follow these expert tips to create a CV that stands out from the crowd.

structure.title: CV Structure
structure.content: Start with your contact information, followed by a professional summary, work experience, education, skills, and additional sections like certifications or languages. Keep it organized and easy to scan.

formatting.title: Formatting Tips
formatting.content: Use a clean, professional font. Keep margins consistent. Use bullet points for readability. Limit your CV to 1-2 pages. Use bold and italics sparingly for emphasis.

keywords.title: Keywords & ATS
keywords.content: Many companies use Applicant Tracking Systems (ATS). Include keywords from the job description. Use standard section headings. Avoid images and complex formatting that ATS can't read.

mistakes.title: Common Mistakes
mistakes.content: Avoid typos and grammatical errors. Don't include irrelevant information. Don't use an unprofessional email address. Don't lie about your experience. Always tailor your CV to the job.

coverLetter.title: Writing a Cover Letter
coverLetter.subtitle: Learn how to write compelling cover letters that complement your CV.
coverLetter.intro: A great cover letter can set you apart from other candidates. It's your chance to show personality and explain why you're the perfect fit.

structure.title: Cover Letter Structure
structure.content: Start with a strong opening that grabs attention. In the body, connect your experience to the job requirements. Close with a clear call to action and express enthusiasm for the role.

personalization.title: Personalization
personalization.content: Address the hiring manager by name when possible. Research the company and mention specific projects or values that resonate with you. Show that you've done your homework.

tone.title: Professional Tone
tone.content: Be professional but approachable. Show enthusiasm without being over the top. Use active language and quantify achievements when possible.

template.title: Sample Template
template.content: Dear [Hiring Manager], I am writing to express my interest in the [Position] role at [Company]. With [X years] of experience in [field], I bring [key skills]. I am particularly drawn to [company aspect]. I look forward to discussing how I can contribute to your team.

attachments.title: Required Attachments
attachments.subtitle: Know what documents to include with your job application.
attachments.intro: Different jobs require different documents. Make sure you have everything ready before applying.

essential.title: Essential Documents
essential.content: CV/Resume, Cover Letter, and References are the core documents most employers expect. Have these ready in PDF format for a professional presentation.

additional.title: Additional Documents
additional.content: Depending on the role, you may need: diplomas and transcripts, professional certifications, portfolio or work samples, letters of recommendation, and proof of identity.

formats.title: File Formats
formats.content: PDF is the gold standard for job applications. It preserves formatting across devices. Name files clearly: FirstName_LastName_CV.pdf. Keep file sizes under 5MB.

organization.title: Staying Organized
organization.content: Create a dedicated folder for each job application. Keep master copies of all documents. Update your CV before each application. Track which version you sent to which employer.
